Title: [Internal] GCash Money-in Onboarding Process
Content:
GCash Onboarding

The GCash Onboarding process provides a simple framework for merchants to integrate with GCash. This document outlines the key steps and requirements necessary for a successful onboarding experience.

Eligibility Criteria

Before starting the onboarding process, ensure your business fits the eligibility criteria set by GCash. The following industry classifications are recognized:

  • Pseudo Pharmaceuticals

  • Government/Public Services and National Defense

  • Lending/Financing

  • Cooperative/Associations

  • Telecommunications

Merchants in certain high-risk industries must undergo additional scrutiny and may require further documentation. The following industries necessitate initial management assessment:

  • Merchants selling regulated products or services

  • Remittance Companies and Foreign Exchange Dealers

  • Multi-Level Marketing

  • Cryptocurrency

  • Pawnshops

Prohibited Industries

GCash does not endorse or allow onboarding for specific industries listed below:

  • Entities engaged in Gambling or Online Gaming

  • Merchants selling illegal substances or drug paraphernalia

  • Merchants involved in the sale of obscene materials

Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) Requirements

Some industries require Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD). The following documents may be necessary:

  • Latest Income Tax Return or Audited Financial Statement

  • Duly Accomplished Due Diligence Form

  • Know-Your-Customer (KYC) Form for primary officers

GCash Features

Upon onboarding, businesses can access various GCash features:

  • GCash Webpay (Money-In)

  • GCash Link and Pay (Tokenization)

Onboarding Process

The onboarding steps involve several critical actions:

  1. Merchant applies through the GCash activation dashboard.

  2. The Merchant Operations (MO) team processes the application.

  3. If approved, GCash confirms via email with a unique Sub-Merchant ID (Sub-MID).

  4. MO activates GCash in the Admin Dashboard.

Post-Onboarding Requests

Merchants can request changes to their business name after the onboarding process. To do this:

  • Fill in the requested changes in a form.

  • Email the change request to GCash, along with necessary documents.

If you have sub-merchants, ensure that their details are updated and communicated promptly.

Conclusion

Successful onboarding with GCash involves meeting specific eligibility requirements and following outlined steps. Make sure to comply with the various documentation standards for your industry.

Frequently Asked Questions

  • What is GCash Onboarding? GCash Onboarding is the process for merchants to integrate their businesses with GCash, involving eligibility checks and specific documentation.

  • Who is eligible for GCash Onboarding? Businesses classified under certain industries like pseudo pharmaceuticals, government services, and telecommunications are eligible.

  • What industries are prohibited from onboarding? Prohibited industries include gambling, illegal substances, and entities selling obscene materials.

  • What are the Enhanced Due Diligence requirements? Enhanced Due Diligence may require documentation such as income tax returns and Know-Your-Customer forms.

  • What are the main steps in the onboarding process? Merchants must apply through the GCash activation dashboard, have their application processed, and receive confirmation from GCash.

Description: Discover the GCash onboarding process. Learn about eligibility criteria, prohibited industries, EDD requirements, and essential steps for merchants.
